title: Colloidal oat melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A soothing bar with colloidal oatmeal and lavender for sensitive and dry skin
excerpt: Colloidal oatmeal is finely milled to remain suspended in the melted base rather than settling to the bottom of the mould. The resulting bar is gently exfoliating and helps calm dry, itchy skin. Lavender essential oil is optional but complements the oat base well.
sourceNotes: Colloidal oatmeal in melt-and-pour soap at 3% of base weight: standard cosmetic craft usage rate. Colloidal vs rolled oats in soap: cosmetic formulation reference -- colloidal oats are pre-milled for suspension; rolled oats settle. Lavender EO stability in M&P base: standard soap-craft reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep colloidal oatmeal at 3% and essential oil at 2% of base weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bars are ready to use once firm -- no cure required. Wrap individually to prevent moisture absorption.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep for one year wrapped or boxed in a cool, dry place. Unwrapped bars will sweat and soften in a humid bathroom.

glossaryTerms.definition: Oats that have been finely milled into a powder small enough to remain suspended in water and soap bases. Used in cosmetics for its soothing, anti-inflammatory action on dry and irritated skin. Not the same as rolled oats, which are too coarse and will sink during a pour.

content.text: The key distinction in this recipe is the type of oat: ordinary rolled or porridge oats will sink to the bottom of the mould before the base sets, producing an uneven distribution and a scratchy bar bottom.
is milled to a consistent fine powder that stays in suspension. Stir it in just before pouring and pour relatively quickly to keep the particles distributed.

items.prepNote: white base gives a cream-coloured bar
3% of base weight; finely milled only

content.text: Cut the soap base into cubes. Melt over a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each. Heat until just melted -- do not boil. Cool to about 60°C before adding other ingredients.

Weigh the colloidal oatmeal and stir directly into the melted base. It will disperse immediately -- colloidal oatmeal is fine enough that pre-mixing into a paste is not necessary. Add the lavender essential oil if using. Stir gently to avoid bubbles.

Pour into the silicone loaf mould immediately after mixing. Any delay will allow the oatmeal to begin settling as the base thickens. Mist the surface with isopropyl alcohol if bubbles form. Set at room temperature for at least two hours before unmoulding. Slice into 6 to 8 bars.

Wrap individually in clingfilm to prevent sweating. Bars are ready to use immediately. They are particularly useful for very dry, eczema-prone, or sensitive skin where a conditioning, soap-base bar is preferred over cold-process.
